differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Renders pages on server or statically at build time for improved performance and SEO.
differences.rightDesc: Generates static HTML by default, minimizing JavaScript shipped to the client.

differences.title: 2. JAVASCRIPT SHIPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Ships JavaScript to make components interactive (hydrates them) on the client side.
differences.rightDesc: Ships minimal or no JavaScript by default, leading to faster initial loads.

differences.title: 3. PRIMARY USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for highly interactive applications, dashboards, and complex user interfaces.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for blogs, marketing sites, documentation, and e-commerce where content is king.

differences.title: 4. DEVELOPMENT PHILOSOPHY
differences.leftDesc: Provides integrated routing, API routes, and image optimization out-of-the-box.
differences.rightDesc: Employs 'Islands Architecture' for selective hydration of UI components.

differences.title: 5. BUILD TOOLING
differences.leftDesc: Leverages Webpack or the newer Turbopack for bundling and development server.
differences.rightDesc: Uses Vite, a fast build tool leveraging native ES modules for quick dev server starts.

differences.title: 6. REACT SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Designed with React in mind, offering seamless integration and Server Components.
differences.rightDesc: Supports React and other UI frameworks as 'integrations' or plugins.

differences.title: 7. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Balances server rendering with client-side interactivity, can lead to larger JS bundles.
differences.rightDesc: Prioritizes extremely fast initial page loads by shipping minimal JavaScript.
